mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below the 8.0 percent threshold signals a structural labor market improvement that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized broad-based demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects accelerating capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% MoM in recovery regimes, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of central bank policy normalization, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY crosses 2.5%, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which systematically supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM growth falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth remains positive while the unemployment rate declines indicates a high-quality expansion with improving labor absorption, which strengthens consumer spending power and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation during a late-cycle expansion signals supply constraints and margin compression, which directly suppresses corporate profitability and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where this supply-demand mismatch occurs, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit in walk-forward validation.
